title: anthoxanthum utriculatum var. altissimum
intro: Anthoxanthum utriculatum var. altissimum, commonly known as tall velvet grass, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family (Poaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of tall velvet grass include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
